label: 387e Annual registration

children.heading: Manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing
children.label: 1 Manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing
children.text: The term “manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing” shall include repackaging or otherwise changing the container, wrapper, or labeling of any tobacco product package in furtherance of the distribution of the tobacco product from the original place of manufacture to the person who makes final delivery or sale to the ultimate consumer or user.

children.heading: Name
children.label: 2 Name
children.text: The term “name” shall include in the case of a partnership the name of each partner and, in the case of a corporation, the name of each corporate officer and director, and the State of incorporation.

children.heading: Registration by owners and operators
children.label: b Registration by owners and operators
children.text: On or before December 31 of each year, every person who owns or operates any establishment in any State engaged in the manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing of a tobacco product or tobacco products shall register with the Secretary the name, places of business, and all such establishments of that person. If enactment of the Family Smoking Prevention and Tobacco Control Act occurs in the second half of the calendar year, the Secretary shall designate a date no later than 6 months into the subsequent calendar year by which registration pursuant to this subsection shall occur.

children.heading: Registration by new owners and operators
children.label: c Registration by new owners and operators
children.text: Every person upon first engaging in the manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing of a tobacco product or tobacco products in any establishment owned or operated in any State by that person shall immediately register with the Secretary that person’s name, place of business, and such establishment.

children.heading: Registration of added establishments
children.label: d Registration of added establishments
children.text: Every person required to register under subsection (b) or (c) shall immediately register with the Secretary any additional establishment which that person owns or operates in any State and in which that person begins the manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing of a tobacco product or tobacco products.

children.heading: Uniform product identification system
children.label: e Uniform product identification system
children.text: The Secretary may by regulation prescribe a uniform system for the identification of tobacco products and may require that persons who are required to list such tobacco products under subsection (i) shall list such tobacco products in accordance with such system.

children.heading: Public access to registration information
children.label: f Public access to registration information
children.text: The Secretary shall make available for inspection, to any person so requesting, any registration filed under this section.

children.heading: Biennial inspection of registered establishments
children.label: g Biennial inspection of registered establishments
children.text: Every establishment registered with the Secretary under this section shall be subject to inspection under or subsection (h), and every such establishment engaged in the manufacture, compounding, or processing of a tobacco product or tobacco products shall be so inspected by 1 or more officers or employees duly designated by the Secretary at least once in the 2-year period beginning with the date of registration of such establishment under this section and at least once in every successive 2-year period thereafter.section 374 of this title

children.heading: Registration by foreign establishments
children.label: h Registration by foreign establishments
children.text: Any establishment within any foreign country engaged in the manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing of a tobacco product or tobacco products, shall register under this section under regulations promulgated by the Secretary. Such regulations shall require such establishment to provide the information required by subsection (i) and shall include provisions for registration of any such establishment upon condition that adequate and effective means are available, by arrangement with the government of such foreign country or otherwise, to enable the Secretary to determine from time to time whether tobacco products manufactured, prepared, compounded, or processed in such establishment, if imported or offered for import into the United States, shall be refused admission on any of the grounds set forth in .section 381(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a tobacco product contained in the applicable list with respect to which a tobacco product standard has been established under or which is subject to , a reference to the authority for the marketing of such tobacco product and a copy of all labeling for such tobacco product;section 387g of this titlesection 387j of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any other tobacco product contained in an applicable list, a copy of all consumer information and other labeling for such tobacco product, a representative sampling of advertisements for such tobacco product, and, upon request made by the Secretary for good cause, a copy of all advertisements for a particular tobacco product; and

children.label: C
children.text: if the registrant filing a list has determined that a tobacco product contained in such list is not subject to a tobacco product standard established under , a brief statement of the basis upon which the registrant made such determination if the Secretary requests such a statement with respect to that particular tobacco product.section 387g of this title

children.heading: Consultation with respect to forms
children.label: 2 Consultation with respect to forms
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of the Treasury in developing the forms to be used for registration under this section to minimize the burden on those persons required to register with both the Secretary and the Tax and Trade Bureau of the Department of the Treasury.

children.heading: Biannual report of any change in product list
children.label: 3 Biannual report of any change in product list
A
children.text: A list of each tobacco product introduced by the registrant for commercial distribution which has not been included in any list previously filed by that person with the Secretary under this subparagraph or paragraph (1). A list under this subparagraph shall list a tobacco product by its established name and shall be accompanied by the other information required by paragraph (1).

children.label: B
children.text: If since the date the registrant last made a report under this paragraph that person has discontinued the manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing for commercial distribution of a tobacco product included in a list filed under subparagraph (A) or paragraph (1), notice of such discontinuance, the date of such discontinuance, and the identity of its established name.

children.label: C
children.text: If since the date the registrant reported under subparagraph (B) a notice of discontinuance that person has resumed the manufacture, preparation, compounding, or processing for commercial distribution of the tobacco product with respect to which such notice of discontinuance was reported, notice of such resumption, the date of such resumption, the identity of such tobacco product by established name, and other information required by paragraph (1), unless the registrant has previously reported such resumption to the Secretary under this subparagraph.

children.label: D
children.text: Any material change in any information previously submitted under this paragraph or paragraph (1).

children.heading: Report preceding introduction of certain substantially equivalent products into interstate commerce
children.label: j Report preceding introduction of certain substantially equivalent products into interstate commerce
i
children.text: the tobacco product is substantially equivalent, within the meaning of , to a tobacco product commercially marketed (other than for test marketing) in the United States as of , or to a tobacco product that the Secretary has previously determined, pursuant to subsection (a)(3) of , is substantially equivalent and that is in compliance with the requirements of this chapter; orsection 387j of this titlesection 387j of this titleFebruary 15, 2007

children.label: ii
children.text: the tobacco product is modified within the meaning of paragraph (3), the modifications are to a product that is commercially marketed and in compliance with the requirements of this chapter, and all of the modifications are covered by exemptions granted by the Secretary pursuant to paragraph (3); and

children.label: B
children.text: action taken by such person to comply with the requirements under that are applicable to the tobacco product.section 387g of this title

children.heading: Application to certain post–, productsFebruary 15, 2007
children.label: 2 Application to certain post–, productsFebruary 15, 2007
children.text: A report under this subsection for a tobacco product that was first introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution in the United States after , and prior to the date that is 21 months after , shall be submitted to the Secretary not later than 21 months after .February 15, 2007June 22, 2009June 22, 2009

children.label: i
children.text: such modification would be a minor modification of a tobacco product that can be sold under this chapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: a report under this subsection is not necessary to ensure that permitting the tobacco product to be marketed would be appropriate for protection of the public health; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an exemption is otherwise appropriate.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: B Regulations
children.text: Not later than 15 months after , the Secretary shall issue regulations to implement this paragraph.June 22, 2009
